text.title: Laravel Queues Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for safe Laravel queue jobs.

rule.statement: Job constructor MUST accept primitive IDs (int, string, UUID), NOT Eloquent models. Reload the model inside `handle()`.
rule.rationale: Serializing models bloats payloads, captures stale state, and fails when soft-deletes change.

rule.statement: Every job's `handle()` MUST be idempotent: check whether the work was already done (e.g. `if ($order->confirmation_sent_at) return;`) before performing the action.
rule.rationale: Queues redeliver on transient failure; non-idempotent handlers double-send, double-charge.

rule.statement: Each job MUST declare `$tries`, `$timeout` (seconds), and `backoff()` (array of delays with jitter). Defaults are forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Default 1-try jobs never retry transients; default infinite-timeout jobs hang the worker pool.

rule.statement: Every job class MUST implement `failed(Throwable $e)` that logs the failure with job context AND notifies operators (Sentry/Slack).
rule.rationale: Silent failures = silent business loss; failed() is the only place to surface them.

rule.statement: Jobs MUST be dispatched to a named queue (`onQueue('mail')` / `onQueue('low')`). The 'default' queue MUST NOT mix priority levels.
rule.rationale: Mixed-priority queues let a slow job (image processing) block a fast job (transactional email); separate queues + pools fix this.
